backend/main.mo: Main backend actor implementing video clip management with CRUD operations, similarity scoring, trending analytics, AI clip suggestions, user profiles, and role-based access control using AccessControl and MixinAuthorization.
feature_evidence.json: Empty feature evidence report JSON file with no features documented.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Text file containing JSON-formatted summaries of all frontend source files including components, hooks, and configuration files.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom OKLCH color system defining CSS variables for light and dark themes including primary, secondary, muted, destructive, and sidebar colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point with title "Beast Clipping" that loads the React application from /src/main.tsx.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React application with routing, authentication flow, profile setup for new users, admin panel access control, and video clipping interface with state management for video selection, clip suggestions, and AI-generated recommendations.
Debug panel component displaying admin authentication status, principal IDs, HashMap size, initialization status, and character-by-character principal comparison with copy-to-clipboard functionality and refresh controls.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React error boundary component that catches and displays errors in the admin panel with detailed error messages, stack traces, component stacks, and recovery options.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin dashboard component displaying statistics (total clips, trending analytics) with a collapsible debug panel, error handling, and retry functionality using React Query.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: Card component displaying a video clip with thumbnail, title, duration, viral score badge, and delete functionality with confirmation dialog.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Displays a scrollable list of saved video clips using ClipCard components, with loading states and empty state messaging.
Shows either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending clips with viral scores, allowing users to select suggestions to populate the clip creation form.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Form component for creating video clips with timestamp inputs (start/end), title field, validation, and save functionality that updates when clips or suggestions are selected.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main layout wrapper with header containing authentication controls (Internet Identity login/logout), conditional admin panel link based on owner status, and extensive debug logging for admin button visibility.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: Profile setup form component that collects user name and email, validates input, and saves to backend using a workaround format (name|email) until backend UserProfile type is updated.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for entering YouTube URLs with validation, video loading, and AI clip generation trigger functionality.
Badge component that displays viral potential scores with color-coded styling and icons based on score thresholds (80+, 50+, <50).
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Simple YouTube video player component that embeds videos using iframe with responsive 16:9 aspect ratio.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Hook that manages backend actor creation with Internet Identity authentication, handles admin token initialization, and invalidates dependent queries on actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminDebug.ts: Hook for fetching admin debug information including current principal, admin status, and placeholder data for backend methods not yet implemented.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Hook that fetches admin statistics including total clips count and trending clips analytics from the backend.
Hook for AI-powered clip generation that currently uses mock data but is designed to integrate with real AI services through backend; includes mutation handling and cache invalidation.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Hook for creating clips with automatic viral score calculation based on duration and title characteristics; handles backend mutation and query invalidation.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: React hook that fetches all video clips using React Query and provides a mutation for deleting clips with loading state tracking.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Hook managing clip timestamp state (start/end minutes/seconds) with validation, formatting, and conversion utilities between time formats.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides Internet Identity authentication context and hooks for ICP, managing login/logout flow, identity state, and AuthClient configuration with delegation support.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: React Query hook that checks if the current authenticated user is an admin by calling the backend's isCallerAdmin method, with extensive debug logging.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Provides React Query hooks for fetching and saving the current user's profile, with automatic cache invalidation on profile updates.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: React Query hook that fetches trending video clips from the backend actor when available.
Utility hook providing functions to validate YouTube URLs and extract video IDs using regex patterns.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for light/dark theme colors using OKLCH color space, including primary, secondary, accent, and chart colors.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React Query and Internet Identity providers, then renders the root App component.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for extracting, storing, and managing URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom color scheme using OKLCH, container settings, animations, and plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Project state file tracking a YouTube video clipping application at version 93 with admin panel authentication transitioning to email-based access control. Contains known issues with admin panel rendering and navigation, plus a comprehensive list of existing features including video URL input, clip selection, AI suggestions, and Internet Identity authentication.
